message:
fix(runtime): guard against post-quit subscription reconciliation and event draining, and scope evidence sink cap to file destinations

program.rs — main loop quit safety:
- Skip initial subscription reconciliation and render_frame() when
  Model::init() returns Cmd::quit() or a termination signal is already
  pending, preventing wasted work and potential panics on torn-down state
- Add `if !self.running { break; }` guards after drain_ready_events(),
  process_subscription_messages(), process_task_results(), and
  process_resize_coalescer() so the main loop exits immediately when
  any of these steps triggers a quit, instead of continuing to render
  or reconcile subscriptions against a shutting-down model
- Guard reconcile_subscriptions() calls behind `if self.running` in
  handle_event(), process_subscription_messages(), and
  process_task_results(), preventing new subscription threads from
  spawning after quit has been requested (these would immediately be
  torn down by shutdown, wasting resources and risking race conditions)

program.rs — screen transition subscription reconciliation:
- Call reconcile_subscriptions() after force-ticking a newly active
  screen in check_screen_transition(), so subscriptions declared by
  the tick handler are started immediately rather than waiting for the
  next event cycle (fixes a bug where screen-transition-triggered
  subscriptions had a one-frame delay)
- Call reconcile_subscriptions() after screen-dispatch update paths
  that use used_screen_dispatch, matching the monolithic model path

program.rs — apply_resize subscription reconciliation:
- Call reconcile_subscriptions() after apply_resize() when the model
  is dirty, so resize-triggered subscription changes take effect
  immediately (previously resize was the only update path that skipped
  reconciliation)

program.rs — drain_ready_events quit:
- Break out of the burst drain loop when self.running becomes false
  after handle_event(), preventing processing of remaining queued
  events after a quit command (in a burst of 8 events with quit at
  event 3, previously all 8 were processed; now only 3 are)

program.rs — tests (582 new lines):
- headless_apply_resize_reconciles_subscriptions: verifies subscription
  count increases after a resize that triggers a subscription
- run_quit_from_init_skips_initial_render_and_subscription_start:
  verifies zero render calls and zero subscription starts when init()
  returns Cmd::quit()
- run_pending_signal_skips_initial_render_and_subscription_start:
  verifies the same for a pre-recorded termination signal
- headless_handle_event_quit_skips_subscription_reconcile: verifies
  that a quit-producing key event does not start new subscriptions
- quit_stops_draining_remaining_burst_events: verifies only quit_after
  events are processed out of a larger burst
- check_screen_transition_reconciles_subscriptions_after_force_tick:
  verifies subscription count increases after a screen transition that
  triggers a subscription via tick_screen()

evidence_sink.rs:
- Add cap_enabled field to EvidenceSinkInner, set to true only for
  File destinations, so stdout sinks never hit the size cap (stdout
  is unbounded by nature; capping it silently drops logs with no
  recovery path)
- Guard both the initial already_capped check and the per-write cap
  check behind cap_enabled
- Rustfmt: collapse single-line metadata chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
